title: Protowoodsia manchuriensis
taxonomy: Protowoodsia manchuriensis is a species of fern in the family Lindsaeaceae. It is native to East Asia and is classified as a vascular plant belonging to the division Pteridophyta. This division also includes other vascular plant species such as mosses and horsetails. Within the division Pteridophyta, Protowoodsia manchuriensis is further classified as a member of the class Polypodiopsida. This classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other fern species.

intro: Protowoodsia manchuriensis, also known as Korean maidenhair, is a species of fern native to East Asia, characterized by its delicate fronds and ability to thrive in shaded, moist environments. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve immune function, and protect against cell damage. Bioactive constituents of Protowoodsia manchuriensis include polysaccharides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often consumed to support overall health and well-being.
